item.title: Project Valhalla, Explained: How a Decade of Work Arrives in JDK 28 - JVM Weekly vol. 180
item.description: The new JVM Weekly is here... and Ragnarok seems to come, as we finally have Valhalla in the JDK. However, situation is a bit... nuanced.

item.title: IN THE WEIGHTS
item.description: The weights are the billions of numbers forming an AI's brain. Type a name and see how strongly the leading AI models recognize it. Are you in the weights?

item.title: I told them forced consent was unlawful. Five years later it cost Elkjop €1.8 million — That Privacy Guy!
item.description: Datatilsynet just fined Elkjop NOK 20 million (€1.8m) for the forced consent in its loyalty club — five years after I told their DPO it was unlawful.
item.author: (Alexander Hanff)

item.title: Compile Zod (30x faster Zod validation)
item.description: Learn how to optimize Zod validation by 30x using aot compilation.

item.title: How Tool Search Works and How It Saves Tokens - Mykhailo Chalyi
item.description: Why agents drown in tool schemas, how tool search fixes it by deferring schemas and loading them on demand, the difference between generic and provider-specific search, and tricks to make it actually good.
item.author: (Mykhailo Chalyi)

item.title: Don't be afraid to comment - Sylvia's Studio
item.description: Yesterday I posted this on Mastodon (post link) I'm building a new habit here on Mastodon: instead of liking, I try to reply to at least 3 posts, especially...

item.title: Is It Time for a New Embedded Linux Build System? — [yoe]
item.description: Edge devices now behave like cloud systems, and software has outgrown cross-compilation. Why a growing class of small teams needs a build system shaped for that job.

item.title: I Bought a 2004 iPod in 2026 · Chris Collins' Notes
item.description: This week I bought an iPod Classic 4th Generation (Mono) and I’m planning on daily driving it. It might seem a strange thing to do, but hear me out.

item.title: Don't test what you don't control | Christian Rackerseder
item.description: Integration and end-to-end tests become unreliable when they depend on services a team does not own. Test your product boundary, not the whole company.

item.title: I almost got hit by a car
item.description: People tend to dismiss safety advice they instinctively feel is overblown. I think that was me, when it came to wearing headphones outside.

item.title: Adding a Town Square - Kev Quirk
item.description: I added a town square to my site. Try it, it's fun!

item.title: ELTIF reporting in five languages
item.description: In multilingual investor reporting, the chain feeding the translation does the damage. Every upstream weakness gets multiplied by language count, on the same publication deadline.
item.author: (Igor Sabol)

item.title: Slow down and sit with it - Yao Yue
item.description: Slow down and sit with it

item.title: ~fpereiro
item.description: “The past months have been mentally exhausting. I’m kind of sick of the entire AI landscape. It all feels pointless.” — Mario Zechner

item.title: I switched to light mode and I haven't been happier – SHTFN
item.description: I felt tired in the past few days. Much of it comes from the combination of work and parenting, sprinkled with the general depression of the outside world. But I also realized I am staring at various screens for most of the day. And all of those screens had one thing in common - dark mode.

item.title: The Token Compression Illusion: Why I'm Skeptical of RTK
item.description: RTK promises dramatic token savings for coding agents, but raw terminal compression is not the same as cheaper, safer, or more accurate software engineering.
item.author: (Przemek Mroczek)

item.title: Submission.Directory · Find where to submit your website.
item.description: A hand-picked directory of website submission sites. Find the best places to submit your website, startup, or product, earn quality backlinks, and rank higher across search engines and AI answers.

item.title: Parliament Lifts Ban on New Nuclear Power Plants
item.description: Things will be bustling again at the Federal Palace in Bern through June 19. Numerous important issues will be on the agenda during the summer session of the National Council and Council of States.
